=== CRYPTO PORTFOLIO RUN — 2026-06-30 ===  (data: TradingView MCP)

Crypto closed a second straight losing quarter with Fear & Greed pinned at 15 (Extreme Fear), and the macro tape is still risk-off — a stalling Clarity Act, JPMorgan likening yield-stablecoins to "shadow banking," and a fresh UK licensing regime keep the Cycle seat bearish across the board. The one clean upgrade overnight is Solana, whose momentum flipped from falling to rising (RSI 38→53, MACD crossed positive) — it's now a watch, one condition away from a trend buy. Uniswap went the other way: its momentum rolled over (RSI below 50), dropping it from last week's buy to a hold, leaving only Aave (full) and Jupiter (starter) clearing the bar today — both comfortably inside the four-name limit that Extreme Fear imposes.

=== BLOCK 2 — Per-Token Verdicts ===

### BTC — HOLD
At $59,430 bitcoin has slipped just below its 200-week moving average ($62,640) — the historical cycle cost-basis floor — in Extreme Fear, but with a death cross active, RSI 32 and no momentum trigger the quorum finds no catalyst. A patient accumulation zone, not yet a buy. Strategy paused new BTC buys and "bitcoin (BTC) steadied near $60,000" after Saylor unveiled buyback plans [source: https://www.theblock.co/post/406568/strategy-mstr-strc-shares-recover-after-brutal-week-saylor-unveils-new-buyback-plans].

Research Desk:
  Technical:   Below all MAs (EMA20 $62,519, SMA50 $68,584, SMA200 $75,403), death cross active, RSI 32.17, MACD hist +5.89 only faintly positive; -38.5% from 52w high, now ~94.9% of the 200wMA.
  On-Chain:    Price has crossed fractionally below the 200-week MA cost-basis floor ($62,640), 210 weekly bars confirmed [source: TradingView data].
  DeFi:        n/a — L1 (no protocol revenue accrual).
  Macro:       Risk-off persists; TD Cowen says the crypto market-structure (Clarity) bill's passage before the midterms is "far from assured" [source: https://www.theblock.co/post/406621/td-cowen-crypto-market-structure-bill-clarity-act].
  Smart Money: Strategy paused BTC purchases, lifted USD reserve to $2.55B and authorized $1B in MSTR buybacks as shares recovered from a record-bad week [source: https://www.theblock.co/post/406568/strategy-mstr-strc-shares-recover-after-brutal-week-saylor-unveils-new-buyback-plans].

Panel:
  Graham (Value):          NEUTRAL — at ~95% of the 200wMA, not the <70% deep discount his margin-of-safety rule requires; no cash-flow anchor.
  Buffett (Quality):       NEUTRAL — dominant monetary network/brand but no revenue to underwrite ownership.
  Dalio (Cycle):           BEARISH — hawkish-Fed/strong-dollar tightening + a stalling Clarity Act is textbook risk-off.
  Druckenmiller (Trend):   BEARISH — death cross active AND RSI 32.17 < 40; downtrend intact (MACD hist only +5.89).
  Burniske (On-chain):     NEUTRAL — no protocol fee capture accrues to holders; valued purely on monetary premium.

Bull: Now trading below the 200-week cost basis in Extreme Fear — historically a high-reward zone for patient capital.
Bear: Death cross + RSI 32 + ETF/macro headwinds mean no momentum or liquidity tailwind yet.

=== TELEGRAM RECAP ===

📊 Daily Crypto Brief — 2026-06-30 (Q2 close)

😨 Fear & Greed: 15 (Extreme Fear) — regime cap 4 buys
🌍 Macro: Still risk-off. A second straight losing quarter; the US market-structure bill looks stalled before the midterms, JPMorgan likened yield-stablecoins to "shadow banking," and the UK finalized a tough new licensing regime. The cycle lens stays bearish across the board.

🟢 BUY
• AAVE — the only major with momentum turning up AND real cash flow: $12.45b deposited, ~$123M/yr revenue, daily buyback. Cooled a touch (RSI 67→63) but the buy is intact. [defillama.com/protocol/aave]
• JUP (small) — best Solana fee machine ($1.53b TVL, ~$412M/yr fees, 50%-of-revenue buyback), but young and barely holding momentum → starter size only. [defillama.com/protocol/jupiter]

🟡 HOLD — cheap, momentum not turned
• ETH — 64% of its 4-year average, but RSI 31 and MACD deeply red.
• SOL ★ — momentum just flipped up (RSI 38→53); one daily close above $75.47 turns this into a buy. Watch it.
• LINK — best moat in the book (Swift, Euroclear, UBS, JPM) at a 58% discount, but RSI 34 — still falling.
• BTC — slipped just under its 4-year cost-basis floor (~$62.6k); a patient accumulation zone, no trigger yet.

⚪ HOLD — can't qualify
• UNI — fundamentals strong (live fee switch + a new $150M institutional deal with Spark), but momentum rolled over (RSI below 50) → dropped from last week's buy. [theblock.co/post/406154]
• HYPE — phenomenal revenue + buyback, but only -15% off its high and too little history to value.
• TON / AERO / PUMP — too little price history (UNKNOWN valuation) → cannot buy.

🚫 Lean avoid: PUMP — no moat, fad-driven revenue, most fragile name in a risk-off tape.
